result.summary: Binance exposes multiple developer-facing trading/payment interfaces: Spot REST, Spot WebSocket Streams, Spot WebSocket API, USDⓈ-M Futures REST, USDⓈ-M Futures WebSocket API, and Binance Pay HTTP APIs; docs also mention a FIX API but it is outside the supported surface types here.
result.description: Binance is a cryptocurrency exchange and payments platform. It provides APIs for spot and derivatives trading, market data streaming, account operations, and merchant crypto payments.

binance_api_key.label: Binance API key and signing secret/private key
binance_api_key.setup: In Binance account [API Management](https://www.binance.com/en/support/faq/360002502072), create an API key for your account. Binance supports `Ed25519` (recommended), `RSA`, and deprecated `HMAC` key types for signed API requests. Enable the needed permissions on the key (for example `TRADE`, `USER_DATA`, `USER_STREAM`, or `FIX_API` / `FIX_API_READ_ONLY` where applicable). Keep the API key plus its corresponding secret or private key material so you can sign requests.

binance_pay_key.label: Binance Pay API identity key and secret key
binance_pay_key.setup: For Binance Pay merchant integrations, contact [Binance Merchant Operation](https://merchant.binance.com/en/help) to set up API authentication credentials. Binance issues an API identity key used as `BinancePay-Certificate-SN` and a secret key used to generate the `BinancePay-Signature` with `HMAC-SHA512`, as described in the [API Common Rules](https://developers.binance.com/legacy-docs/binance-pay/api-common).

surfaces.name: Binance Spot REST API
surfaces.notes: Public market-data endpoints exist with security type `NONE`, and market-data-only traffic can use `https://data-api.binance.vision`; authenticated trading/account/user-stream management endpoints require signed requests.
